Lumayo Holdings, Inc. — DPA Schedule and Cross-Border Transfer Memo (PH), Ref: LHI-DPA-2026-07, Effective 03 June 2026.
Scope: HR onboarding, payroll/tax filings (PH tax authority), banking disbursements, and local exchange disclosure workstreams; processing is under RA 10173 (Data Privacy Act) and SRC insider trading rules.
Personal data categories: employee name, corporate email, masked TIN (last four only), bank account alias and masked number (XXXX-4472); no passports are collected for project-based consultants.
Minimisation: onboarding forms revised 01 May 2026 to drop full TIN and mother’s maiden name fields; biometric capture is disabled and not in use.
Retention: payroll and tax support records retained for 5 years from end of taxable year per local practice, then queued for erasure within 90 days; marketing consents are reviewed annually.
DSAR log: Mr. Ramon I. Querubin requested access and correction on 15 May 2026; copies provided on 18 May 2026, with bank token rotated and outdated mailing address deleted.
Consent withdrawal: Ms. Teresa D. Llorente withdrew marketing consent on 12 Apr 2026; status closed and suppression list updated—no further outreach is scheduled.
Cross-border transfer: HR analytics limited to masked payroll data exported to Kawayan Analytics Pte. Ltd. (SG) under Data Processing Agreement LHI-KA-2026, approved by DPO on 02 Apr 2026.
Transfer impact assessment completed and filed in the privacy regulator logbook (NDSC Ref: LHI-2026-14); encryption at rest and in transit required; no special categories or biometrics included in the export.
Control note (negated): There is currently no cross-border transfer of trading desk chat transcripts or MNPI to any third party; such transfers remain prohibited.
MNPI handling: unaudited Q2 figures and local exchange disclosure drafts (‘Project Kapiz’) are ring-fenced; access restricted to Capital Markets and Legal per SRC concepts on insider trading, with blackout enforced.
Incident status: no privacy incidents or reportable MNPI leaks were recorded in Q2 to date; the last risk assessment closed with no corrective actions.
Data processors: IslaPay Rural Bank (payroll disbursements) and CloudBamboo PH, Inc. (HRIS) operate under signed DPAs; subprocessor changes require 10-day employee notice.
Points of contact: Atty. Liza M. Cabral (Compliance) and Ms. Pilar S. Nogales (DPO), privacy@lumayo-holdings.ph, tel. +63 28 555 0199 (fictional).
This memo documents existing controls only and does not, by itself, authorize any new processing; prior export approval LHI-KA-2026 remains valid until 31 Dec 2026 unless revoked.
